commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

Security hardening round 1: close auth bypass, add rate limiting and headers

CRITICAL fixes:
- Remove X-User-Id header auth bypass from guardar.php, cargar.php, audit.php, arca.php
- All API endpoints now require PHP session auth only (no header fallback)
- Remove X-User-Id from CORS allowed headers and audit log fallback
- Remove X-User-Id header sending from frontend JS (3 locations)

- Hide AFIP certificate paths from error responses (log to error_log instead)
- Sanitize error messages to not reflect user input (arca.php action param)

- Add login rate limiting: max 5 attempts per IP+email, 15 min lockout
- Auto-clear rate limit on successful login

Security headers (both sites):
- Add Content-Security-Policy with strict whitelist
- Add Permissions-Policy (block camera, mic, geo, payment)
- Block .git directory access (RedirectMatch 404)
- Block .sql, .log, .env, .bak, .swp on institutional site
- Remove deprecated X-XSS-Protection header

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add SS304 Colour Physics tutorial (Chapter 7)

Three interactive canvases:
1. Thin-film interference colour spectrum strip (0–175 nm, labelled zones)
2. Peak temperature → cooling curve → oxide thickness → colour swatch
3. Power × speed grid map with crosshair + live colour swatch + readout

Physics model: Arrhenius k_p = 3.9e9 × exp(−12400/T) nm²/s, parabolic
growth integrated over exponential cooling (τ = 4s, calibrated to match
spec: 18% power, 200mm/s → ~91 nm → Deep Blue).

Also adds Chapter 7 card to learning-lasers/index.html.

commit.message:
fix(invites): remove token hashing to fix broken invite links

The invite system had a double-hash bug: GET list endpoint used hashed
tokens in URLs, but the accept endpoint hashed them again, making all
admin-panel invite links fail with "Invitation introuvable".

- Store raw tokens in BusinessInvite.token instead of SHA256 hash
- Accept endpoint compares raw tokens directly
- Register stores raw inviteToken in pendingInviteToken
- Expired all existing PENDING invites (had hashed tokens)
- Also: ProHomeClient now sends legalForm/nafCode/nafLabel on create

commit.message:
fix: add operation queue to simple-server.js (the one Railway actually runs)

Railway runs simple-server.js, not server.js. Previous mutex was in
operationHandler.js which is only used by server.js. This wraps
_executeOperation in a per-graph promise queue so concurrent operations
on the same graph are serialized.

perf(infra): right-size Longhorn CPU guarantees from 12% to 3%

foundry-core. Also adds explicit resource limits for longhornManager
in the ArgoCD inline values for consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(unified): use module_integrity for L4 attestation level

The legacy file_integrity check treats server-only files (not present
on client) as "missing" and fails the check. The unified module_integrity
properly handles these by excluding them.

This fix:
- Updates L4 attestation to prefer module_integrity.valid
- Falls back to legacy file_integrity if module_integrity unavailable
- Updates degradation detection to use same logic

Fixes issue where 1140/1140 disk files matching registry still showed
"Issues Found" and 3/5 attestation due to 24 server-only files in manifest.

Bump to v1.1.18.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(py): Python interpreter provisioning from python-build-standalone (#827)

Adds automatic Python interpreter provisioning from

[python-build-standalone](https://github.com/astral-sh/python-build-standalone)
(PBS), replacing the need for users to manually specify interpreter URLs
and
checksums or rely on `rules_python`'s version manifests.

Interpreters are discovered automatically from PBS release artifacts and
cached
in `MODULE.bazel.lock` via the Bazel facts API. The root module owns all
configuration — release dates, mirror URLs, pre-release policy, default
version
— while dependency modules can request Python versions additively.

### Usage

```starlark
interpreters = use_extension("@aspect_rules_py//py/unstable:extension.bzl", "python_interpreters")
interpreters.configure(
    releases = ["20260303", "20241002"],
)
interpreters.toolchain(python_version = "3.13", is_default = True)
interpreters.toolchain(python_version = "3.11")
interpreters.toolchain(python_version = "3.15", pre_release = True)

use_repo(interpreters, "python_interpreters")
register_toolchains("@python_interpreters//...")
```

### Module scoping

| Setting | Root module | Non-root module |
|---|---|---|
| `configure()` | Sets release search space and mirror | Silently
ignored |
| `toolchain(python_version)` | Adds to global set | Adds to global set
|
| `toolchain(is_default)` | Honored | Silently ignored |
| `toolchain(pre_release)` | Honored | Silently ignored |

### Changes are visible to end-users: yes

- Searched for relevant documentation and updated as needed: yes
- Breaking change (forces users to change their own code or config): no
- Suggested release notes appear below: yes

New `python_interpreters` module extension at
`//py/unstable:extension.bzl` for
automatic Python interpreter provisioning from python-build-standalone.
Supports
release-date-based versioning, facts API caching, freethreaded builds,
pre-release version policies, and root-module-scoped configuration.

### Test plan

provenance test

comes from `python_3_*` repos, not `rules_python`

no bleed, icons render sharp at any opacity.

Fix refresh_index page matching to handle all CustomGPT API field formats

The old code only matched pages by p.filename which returned deleted: 0

plus suffix matching for URL-prefixed values.

to manage 50+ clients as "projects" instead of using the Partner program.

- Professional: 15 projects, 25 subcontractors
- Business: 30 projects, 50 subcontractors
- Enterprise: unlimited

with billing context checks. Upgrade messages mention Partner program.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Filter transfer leaves per-receiver in queryTransfers for MIMO (#5634)

## Summary

Filters `TransferLeaf` results per-receiver when querying pending
transfers in MIMO mode. Previously, `queryTransfers` returned all leaves
in a multi-receiver transfer regardless of which receiver was querying,
causing the SDK to fail when attempting to
`decryptEcies(leaf.secretCipher)` for leaves encrypted for other
receivers.

Adds `MarshalProtoForReceiver` method to `Transfer` that accepts an
optional receiver identity pubkey. When provided (and MIMO mode is
active), it filters `TransferLeaves` to only those whose
`transfer_receiver_id` matches the `TransferReceiver` with the given
identity pubkey. The existing `MarshalProto` remains unchanged — it
delegates to a shared `marshalWithLeaves` helper.

### Technical Notes

Only the `queryTransfers` call site needs the new method — it already
has `walletIdentityPubkey` and `useMIMO` available. All other
`MarshalProto` call sites (14 across 6 handler files) are
sender-initiated, SSP, or internal and don't need receiver filtering.
When the receiver pubkey doesn't match any `TransferReceiver` (sender
queries, legacy transfers), all leaves are returned as before.

## Test Plan

- E2E validated via Spark MCP: created 3 wallets, funded sender with 50k
sats, sent multi-receiver transfer (15k + 10k), both receivers
independently claimed their leaves and ended with correct balances (15k,
10k, 25k remaining for sender)
- `golangci-lint run --new-from-rev=HEAD` passes with 0 issues
- `go build ./so/ent/... ./so/handler/...` compiles cleanly

<details>
<summary>E2E Proof of Work</summary>

1. Created 3 wallets (sender, receiver A, receiver B)
2. Funded sender: `spark_deposit` → 50,000 sats
3. Sent multi-transfer: `spark_send_multi_transfer` → 15k to A, 10k to B
(transfer ID: `019cd941-49f7-7ca2-93e4-3cec93d30d92`)
4. Receiver A claim: `spark_claim_transfers` → balance 15,000 sats ✅
5. Receiver B claim: `spark_claim_transfers` → claimed 1 transfer,
balance 10,000 sats ✅
6. Sender balance: 25,000 sats ✅ (50k - 15k - 10k)

</details>

Resolves SPARK-485

---------
